name.reasoning: Hindi name transliterated to English. No medical coding applicable.

age.reasoning: 'वर्ष' means 'years' in Hindi. Age is 67 years.

diagnoses.standardized_english_term: Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus (15 years duration, poorly controlled)
diagnoses.reasoning: 'टाइप 2 मधुमेह' translates to 'Type 2 Diabetes Mellitus'. Given concurrent CKD Stage 3b, the more specific ICD-10 code E11.65 (Type 2 Diabetes with CKD) is appropriate. HbA1c of 10.2% confirms poor control.
'कोरोनरी आर्टरी डिजीज' is a direct Hindi transliteration of 'Coronary Artery Disease'. Unambiguous mapping.
'क्रोनिक किडनी रोग' translates to 'Chronic Kidney Disease', with 'Stage 3b' in English. eGFR of 32 mL/min/1.73m² is consistent with CKD Stage 3b (30-44).

Blood pressure reading 142/86 mmHg exceeds hypertension thresholds (≥140/90 systolic). While not explicitly listed as a diagnosis, the elevated BP in a 67-year-old with CKD and CAD strongly suggests hypertension. Confidence slightly reduced as it is inferred from vitals rather than explicitly stated.

medications.reasoning: 'इंसुलिन ग्लार्जिन' is a Hindi transliteration of 'Insulin Glargine'. 'रात को' means 'at night/bedtime'. Basal insulin for poorly controlled T2DM.

medications.original_text: इंसुलिन लिस्प्रो — भोजन के साथ (8-10 यूनिट)
medications.reasoning: 'इंसुलिन लिस्प्रो' is Hindi transliteration of 'Insulin Lispro'. 'भोजन के साथ' means 'with meals'. Rapid-acting insulin for prandial coverage.

medications.standardized_english_term: Empagliflozin 10 mg in the morning (if eGFR >30)
medications.reasoning: 'एम्पाग्लिफ्लोज़िन' is Hindi transliteration of 'Empagliflozin'. Note documents conditional use based on eGFR >30; patient's eGFR is 32, so just above threshold — requires monitoring.

medications.original_text: एटोरवास्टेटिन 20 मिग्रा — रात को (खुराक कम — CKD)
medications.standardized_english_term: Atorvastatin 20 mg at bedtime (dose reduced for CKD)
medications.reasoning: 'एटोरवास्टेटिन' is Hindi transliteration of 'Atorvastatin'. Note indicates dose reduction due to CKD. 'रात को' means 'at night'.
'एस्पिरिन' is Hindi transliteration of 'Aspirin'. 'प्रतिदिन' means 'daily'. Standard antiplatelet dose for CAD.
'ओमेप्राज़ोल' is Hindi transliteration of 'Omeprazole'. 'गैस्ट्रो-सुरक्षा' means 'gastroprotection', indicating use for GI protection concurrent with aspirin therapy.

vitals.reasoning: 'रक्तचाप' means 'blood pressure'. Systolic component 142 mmHg is above the 140 mmHg threshold for hypertension.
Diastolic component from the same blood pressure reading. 86 mmHg is below 90 threshold but elevated.

lab_values.interpretation: Critically above target; 'बहुत खराब' means 'very poor'. Indicates severely uncontrolled diabetes.
lab_values.reasoning: HbA1c is in English. Value of 10.2% is far above the <7.0% target, consistent with the clinical note stating poorly controlled diabetes for 15 years.

lab_values.interpretation: Critically above normal (approximately 248 mg/dL). Severely elevated.
lab_values.reasoning: 'उपवास रक्त शर्करा' translates to 'fasting blood sugar/glucose'. 13.8 mmol/L is approximately 2.5 times the upper limit of normal.

lab_values.interpretation: Significantly elevated (approximately 2.24 mg/dL). Consistent with CKD Stage 3b.
lab_values.reasoning: 'क्रिएटिनिन' is Hindi transliteration of 'Creatinine'. Value nearly double the upper reference limit, consistent with the documented CKD Stage 3b.

lab_values.interpretation: Below normal. Consistent with CKD Stage 3b (30-44 mL/min). Borderline for empagliflozin use (>30 required).
lab_values.reasoning: eGFR is in English. Value of 32 falls within the CKD Stage 3b range (30-44). Critically close to the threshold for continuing empagliflozin.

lab_values.interpretation: Elevated (hyperkalemia). 'सावधानी' means 'caution'. Clinically significant in setting of CKD — risk of cardiac arrhythmia.
lab_values.reasoning: 'पोटेशियम' is Hindi transliteration of 'Potassium'. Value 5.4 mEq/L exceeds the upper reference of 5.0. Hyperkalemia in CKD context requires attention.

lab_values.interpretation: Severely elevated (>22.6x upper limit). Indicates significant albuminuria consistent with diabetic nephropathy and CKD progression.
lab_values.reasoning: 'मूत्र में प्रोटीन' means 'protein in urine'. The clinical note mentions '++ albuminuria' and the lab CSV provides the quantitative Urine ACR of 68 mg/mmol. These are correlated findings mapped to the same standard measurement.

lab_values.interpretation: Low-normal. Borderline in the context of CKD (may indicate early protein wasting).
lab_values.reasoning: 'एल्ब्यूमिन' is Hindi transliteration of 'Albumin'. Value is within reference range but at the lower boundary. Not in the OMOP reference table provided; OMOP concept ID set to null.

lab_values.interpretation: Below normal. Anaemia, likely renal anaemia secondary to CKD Stage 3b (reduced erythropoietin production).
lab_values.reasoning: Hb is an abbreviation for Haemoglobin. Value of 10.8 g/dL is well below the male reference range of 13-17 g/dL, indicating moderate anaemia consistent with CKD.

flags: CRITICAL: HbA1c 10.2% indicates severely uncontrolled type 2 diabetes despite insulin therapy — urgent therapeutic escalation needed
CRITICAL: Hyperkalemia (K+ 5.4 mEq/L) in CKD patient — cardiac monitoring required; avoid potassium-sparing agents
WARNING: eGFR 32 mL/min is borderline for empagliflozin use (threshold >30) — close monitoring required; consider discontinuation if eGFR declines further
WARNING: Significant albuminuria (Urine ACR 68 mg/mmol) suggests progressive diabetic nephropathy — consider ACEi/ARB if not contraindicated by hyperkalemia
WARNING: Moderate anaemia (Hb 10.8 g/dL) likely renal anaemia secondary to CKD — consider erythropoiesis-stimulating agent evaluation
WARNING: Systolic BP 142 mmHg above target for CKD/diabetic patient — antihypertensive therapy not currently prescribed; consider adding ACEi/ARB (with K+ monitoring) or calcium channel blocker
NOTE: No ACEi/ARB in medication list despite CKD with albuminuria and CAD — may be omitted due to hyperkalemia risk; needs clinical review
NOTE: Fasting glucose 13.8 mmol/L (≈248 mg/dL) corroborates poor glycaemic control
